heading: Gwindinup vs Hacketts Gully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gwindinup, Western Australia and Hacketts Gully, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gwindinup has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Hacketts Gully (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gwindinup or Hacketts Gully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gwindinup has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Hacketts Gully scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gwindinup scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Hacketts Gully scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gwindinup or Hacketts Gully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gwindinup has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
